title: MiFID II Transaction Reporting — Field Population Quality Scorecards by Counterparty

compliance.assurance_rationale: MiFID II Art.26 — UC provides continuous Splunk telemetry evidence for this regulatory requirement, uplifted from contributing to partial after review confirmed the SPL captures relevant monitoring signals.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that MiFID II Art.26 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.5.24: MiFID II Transaction Reporting — Field Population Quality Scorecards by Counterparty.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.5.24' running on index=reg sourcetype="arm:submission" (trade_id, venue, counterparty, reject_reason, field_nulls_json, _time), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=reg sourcetype="arm:submission" earliest=-30d
| spath path=field_nulls_json
| eval null_cnt=mvcount('field_nulls_json{}')
| where isnotnull(reject_reason) OR null_cnt>3
| stats count as bad_msgs, avg(null_cnt) as avg_nulls by counterparty
| sort - bad_msgs
description: EMIR/MiFIR reporting quality depends on complete fields. Scoring rejects and nulls in critical RTS 22 fields by executing broker or desk prevents silent degradation of report quality after system upgrades.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for MiFID II compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes reg and sourcetypes arm:submission. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Normalize `field_nulls_json` to multivalue; (2) threshold per message type; (3) join to release calendar; (4) weekly quality stand-up; (5) evidence for NCAs inquiries. Indexes required: `index=reg`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=arm:submission`.
visualization: Table (counterparties), Line chart (bad_msgs over time), Single value (total).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: ARM platform logs via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=reg` `sourcetype="arm:submission"` (trade_id, venue, counterparty, reject_reason, field_nulls_json, _time).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize `field_nulls_json` to multivalue; (2) threshold per message type; (3) join to release calendar; (4) weekly quality stand-up; (5) evidence for NCAs inquiries.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=reg sourcetype="arm:submission" earliest=-30d
| spath path=field_nulls_json
| eval null_cnt=mvcount('field_nulls_json{}')
| where isnotnull(reject_reason) OR null_cnt>3
| stats count as bad_msgs, avg(null_cnt) as avg_nulls by counterparty
| sort - bad_msgs
```

Understanding this SPL

**MiFID II Transaction Reporting — Field Population Quality Scorecards by Counterparty** — EMIR/MiFIR reporting quality depends on complete fields. Scoring rejects and nulls in critical RTS 22 fields by executing broker or desk prevents silent degradation of report quality after system upgrades.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=reg` `sourcetype="arm:submission"` (trade_id, venue, counterparty, reject_reason, field_nulls_json, _time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): ARM platform logs via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: reg; **sourcetype**: arm:submission. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=reg, sourcetype="arm:submission", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Extracts structured paths (JSON/XML) with `spath`.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **null_cnt** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(reject_reason) OR null_cnt>3` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by counterparty** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (counterparties), Line chart (bad_msgs over time), Single value (total).
